template_name: Standard Prescription Format - Seizure Mimics in a Neonate

condition.name: Seizure Mimics in a Neonate
overview: Not all abnormal movements in neonates are seizures.
Many benign or non-epileptic events resemble seizures but have distinct features that help differentiate them.
Recognizing seizure mimics avoids over-diagnosis, unnecessary medications, hospitalization, and parental distress.

key_features: Stops with passive flexion or holding
Triggered by tactile or auditory stimuli
Facial twitches or smiles during REM sleep
Moro reflex or startle during handling
No progression and no abnormal EEG
Symmetric extension then flexion of limbs
Occurs in response to sudden stimulus

common_mimics.condition: Apnea with color change / BRUE-like events

clues_favoring_mimic: Suppressible with touch, holding, or repositioning
No autonomic signs in most cases
Normal feeding, tone, growth, and neurological examination
clues_favoring_true_seizure: Does not stop with holding or repositioning
history_template: Triggering events such as sound, touch, feeding, or sleep
Response to comforting, repositioning, or stimulation
Whether movements occur during sleep or wakefulness
Family history of neonatal seizures or hyperekplexia
examination_template: Observe movement characteristics directly or via video
Check whether movement is provoked or spontaneous
Assess whether movement stops with passive flexion or holding
Look for facial twitching, ocular movements, limb jerks
Assess during sleep and awake states

features: Triggered by tactile or auditory stimuli
May have apnea or fall-like posture
hyperekplexia.treatment_note: May respond to CLONAZEPAM if symptomatic

features: Facial twitches or smiles in REM sleep

investigations_if_red_flags_present.name: GENETIC TESTING
investigations_if_red_flags_present.purpose: If hyperekplexia or recurrent unexplained events suspected

suspected_true_seizure: Treat as per neonatal seizure protocol

parent_counseling: Many newborn movements are normal or benign. — नवजात शिशु की कई हलचलें सामान्य होती हैं।
Seizure mimics do not usually cause brain damage. — ये दौरे जैसी हलचलें आमतौर पर दिमाग को नुकसान नहीं पहुँचाती।
Avoid unnecessary panic or medication without confirmation. — बिना पुष्टि के घबराएं नहीं और दवा शुरू न करें।
Observe for red flags such as apnea, cyanosis, feeding difficulty, eye deviation, or persistent rhythmic movements. — सांस रुकना, नीलापन, दूध न पीना, आंखों का टेढ़ा होना जैसे लक्षणों पर ध्यान दें।
Most mimics resolve with time. — ये अधिकतर अपने आप ठीक हो जाती हैं।

summary_table.clue_features: Only during sleep, stops on arousal

flow_logic: If rhythmic, not stimulus-sensitive, and associated with altered tone or consciousness, suspect seizure
If stimulus-sensitive, suppressible, or occurs during sleep only, likely seizure mimic
If diagnosis remains uncertain, arrange VIDEO-EEG or specialist referral
quick_checklist_for_opd: Is the movement rhythmic or jerky?
Does it occur during sleep or wakefulness?
Is the baby feeding well and neurologically normal?
Any family history or red flags?

standard_prescription_format.title: Standard Prescription - Seizure Mimics in a Neonate
provisional_diagnosis_options: Seizure mimic - benign neonatal sleep myoclonus
Abnormal movement - rule out neonatal seizure
investigation_orders: No routine investigations if classic benign mimic and normal exam
SEPSIS / METABOLIC WORK-UP if clinically indicated

medication_template.drug_name: No routine anticonvulsant required for benign mimic
medication_template.instructions_en: Do not start seizure medicines unless true seizure is suspected or confirmed
medication_template.instructions_hi: सही दौरे का संदेह या पुष्टि बिना एंटी-सीज़र दवा शुरू न करें

medication_template.instructions_en: Only if hyperekplexia is diagnosed and symptomatic, under specialist advice
medication_template.instructions_hi: केवल हाइपेरेक्प्लेक्सिया की पुष्टि और लक्षण होने पर विशेषज्ञ की सलाह से

general_advice: Ask parents to record video if possible
Reassure if features are consistent with benign mimic

warning_signs: Apnea with bradycardia or cyanosis — सांस रुकना, धड़कन धीमी होना या नीलापन
Eye deviation or lip smacking — आंखों का टेढ़ा होना या होंठ चटकाना
Persistent tonic or clonic movements — लगातार अकड़न या झटके
Non-suppressible movements — पकड़ने पर भी न रुकने वाली हलचल
Poor feeding or lethargy — दूध न पीना या सुस्ती
Abnormal neurological examination — असामान्य तंत्रिका जांच
expected_course: Benign neonatal sleep myoclonus resolves spontaneously by 3-6 months. Jitteriness usually resolves once metabolic cause (hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia) is corrected. Hyperekplexia may require long-term medication. Video-EEG helps differentiate true seizures from mimics. Most mimics have an excellent prognosis.
key_clinical_points: Ask parents to record a video of the episode for review
Stimulus-sensitive and suppressible movements favor mimic over seizure
Normal interictal EEG and neurological exam are reassuring
Do NOT start anticonvulsants without confirmed seizure diagnosis
Benign sleep myoclonus occurs ONLY during sleep and stops on arousal
Jitteriness may indicate underlying metabolic derangement requiring correction

severity_assessment.mild: Isolated jitteriness or brief myoclonic jerks during sleep with normal exam — observation and parental reassurance
severity_assessment.moderate: Recurrent episodes with parental anxiety, uncertain diagnosis — video documentation, metabolic screen, neurology follow-up
severity_assessment.severe: Non-suppressible rhythmic movements, eye deviation, apnea, altered tone — urgent EEG and full neonatal seizure workup, consider admission
